phase.name: Infrastructure Timing Module
phase.description:
Create the Python module that parses shell-generated timing data (key=value timestamps from SkyPilot setup: blocks), computes durations and cost analysis, and logs everything to MLflow.
This is implemented FIRST (before modifying SkyPilot YAMLs) because: 1. Tests can run locally without cloud GPU instances 2. The module can handle missing timing files gracefully 3. Once the module exists, YAML changes are a simple addition

task.issue_title: feat(observability): add infrastructure timing parser + cost analysis module
task.description:
Create src/minivess/observability/infrastructure_timing.py with:
1. parse_setup_timing(timing_file: Path) -> dict[str, float] Reads key=value timestamp file (e.g., "python_install_start=1710412800.123") and computes durations for each operation.
2. compute_cost_analysis( setup_seconds: float, training_seconds: float, epoch_count: int, hourly_rate_usd: float, ) -> dict[str, float] Computes: total_cost, setup_cost, training_cost, effective_gpu_rate, setup_fraction, gpu_utilization_fraction, epochs_to_amortize_setup, break_even_epochs.
3. log_infrastructure_timing(tracker: ExperimentTracker) -> None Reads timing_setup.txt from cwd, parses it, logs all setup_* params to MLflow. Also logs the timing file as a "timing" artifact.
4. log_cost_analysis(tracker: ExperimentTracker, ...) -> None Computes cost analysis and logs cost_* metrics at step=0 to MLflow. Reads INSTANCE_HOURLY_USD from env var (default 0.0 for local).
5. generate_timing_jsonl( setup_durations: dict, training_seconds: float, epoch_count: int, hourly_rate: float ) -> str Generates JSONL content for the timing artifact.
Design decisions (from reviewer feedback): - Shell timing uses key=value format (no JSON in bash, no bc dependency) - Cost values logged as MLflow metrics at step=0 (not params) - Setup timing logged as MLflow params (one-time values) - First/steady epoch timing uses prof_ prefix (not setup_) - cost_instance_sku (not cost_instance_type) to avoid sys_gpu_model overlap - Graceful no-op when timing file doesn't exist (local runs)

tdd_spec.red: - tests/v2/unit/test_infrastructure_timing.py::test_parse_setup_timing_basic -> Parse a sample timing file with 3 operations, verify durations correct - test_parse_setup_timing_missing_end -> Missing end timestamp for an operation -> duration is None, no crash - test_parse_setup_timing_empty_file -> Empty file -> empty dict, no crash - test_parse_setup_timing_file_not_found -> Non-existent file -> empty dict, no crash - test_compute_cost_analysis_basic -> 600s setup, 1750s training, 50 epochs, $0.40/hr -> total_cost ~= 0.261, setup_fraction ~= 0.255, effective_gpu_rate ~= 0.537 - test_compute_cost_analysis_zero_training -> 0s training -> effective_gpu_rate = -1.0 (sentinel), no ZeroDivisionError - test_compute_cost_analysis_zero_hourly_rate -> $0.0/hr (local) -> all costs = 0.0, no errors - test_compute_cost_analysis_amortization -> 600s setup, 30s/epoch: epochs_to_amortize = 181 (9*600/30 + 1) - test_compute_cost_analysis_break_even -> 600s setup, 35s/epoch: break_even = 18 (600/35 + 1) - test_generate_timing_jsonl_format -> Output is valid JSONL (each line parses as JSON) - test_generate_timing_jsonl_has_cost_summary -> Last line has "operation": "cost_summary" with all cost fields - test_log_infrastructure_timing_no_file -> log_infrastructure_timing() with no timing file -> no-op, no crash - test_log_infrastructure_timing_logs_params -> Mock ExperimentTracker, verify setup_* params logged - test_log_cost_analysis_logs_metrics -> Mock ExperimentTracker, verify cost_* metrics logged at step=0 - test_hourly_rate_from_env -> INSTANCE_HOURLY_USD=0.40 in env -> hourly_rate = 0.40 - test_hourly_rate_default_zero -> No INSTANCE_HOURLY_USD in env -> hourly_rate = 0.0
tdd_spec.green: - Create src/minivess/observability/infrastructure_timing.py - Implement parse_setup_timing() - Implement compute_cost_analysis() - Implement log_infrastructure_timing() - Implement log_cost_analysis() - Implement generate_timing_jsonl()

phase.name: SkyPilot YAML Timing
phase.description:
Add shell-level timing (key=value timestamps via date +%s.%N) to all SkyPilot YAML setup: blocks. The run: block reads the timing file and calls log_infrastructure_timing() before starting training.
Also add INSTANCE_HOURLY_USD env var injection in launcher scripts.

task.issue_title: feat(cloud): add shell timing to SkyPilot YAML setup blocks
task.description:
Modify dev_runpod.yaml and smoke_test_lambda.yaml setup: blocks to emit key=value timestamps for every major operation: - python_install (start/end) - uv_install (start/end) - uv_sync (start/end) - dvc_config (start/end) - dvc_pull (start/end) - model_weights (start/end) - verification (start/end) - setup (start/end — overall)
Modify run: blocks to call the timing logger before training: ```bash python -c " from minivess.observability.infrastructure_timing import log_infrastructure_timing_standalone log_infrastructure_timing_standalone() " ```
Add INSTANCE_HOURLY_USD to .env.example (Rule #22).
Modify launcher scripts to inject INSTANCE_HOURLY_USD from SkyPilot status after provisioning (best-effort — may not always be available).

tdd_spec.red: - tests/v2/unit/test_skypilot_yaml_timing.py::test_dev_runpod_yaml_has_timing_start -> Parse dev_runpod.yaml, verify setup: block contains "setup_start=$(date" - test_dev_runpod_yaml_has_timing_end -> Parse dev_runpod.yaml, verify setup: block contains "setup_end=$(date" - test_dev_runpod_yaml_has_all_operations_timed -> Verify all 7 operations have start/end timestamp lines - test_smoke_test_lambda_yaml_has_timing -> Parse smoke_test_lambda.yaml, verify timing lines present - test_env_example_has_instance_hourly_usd -> Parse .env.example, verify INSTANCE_HOURLY_USD is present - test_run_block_calls_timing_logger -> Parse dev_runpod.yaml run: block, verify timing logger call
tdd_spec.green: - Modify deployment/skypilot/dev_runpod.yaml setup: block with timing - Modify deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_lambda.yaml setup: block with timing - Modify dev_runpod.yaml run: block to call timing logger - Modify smoke_test_lambda.yaml run: block to call timing logger - Add INSTANCE_HOURLY_USD to .env.example - Modify scripts/launch_dev_runpod.py to inject hourly cost env var

phase.name: Train Flow Integration
phase.description: Integrate infrastructure timing into train_flow.py so that every training run automatically logs setup timing and cost analysis. Also add first/steady epoch timing to trainer.py.

task.issue_title: feat(training): integrate infrastructure timing into train_flow.py
task.description:
Modify train_flow.py to: 1. Call log_infrastructure_timing(tracker) at the start of the run (reads timing_setup.txt if it exists, no-op otherwise) 2. Call log_cost_analysis(tracker, ...) at the end of training (after all folds complete, before run is finalized)
Modify trainer.py to: 1. Record prof_first_epoch_seconds (epoch 0 wall time) 2. Record prof_steady_epoch_seconds (epoch 1 wall time) 3. Log these as MLflow metrics at step=0 and step=1
These changes are minimal — just two function calls in train_flow.py and two timing measurements in trainer.py.

tdd_spec.red: - tests/v2/unit/test_train_flow_timing.py::test_train_flow_calls_infrastructure_timing -> AST-parse train_flow.py (ast.walk, NOT regex — Rule #16), verify log_infrastructure_timing is called - test_train_flow_calls_cost_analysis -> AST-parse train_flow.py, verify log_cost_analysis is called - test_trainer_records_first_epoch_time -> Verify trainer.py has prof_first_epoch_seconds timing - test_trainer_records_steady_epoch_time -> Verify trainer.py has prof_steady_epoch_seconds timing
tdd_spec.green: - Add log_infrastructure_timing() call to train_flow.py - Add log_cost_analysis() call to train_flow.py - Add epoch 0/1 timing to trainer.py fit() method

phase.name: DuckDB Extraction
phase.description: Extend the DuckDB extraction pipeline to include setup_* and cost_* data in queryable Parquet tables. Add cost_* to the metric exclusion list so they don't appear as training curves.

task.issue_title: feat(analytics): extract infrastructure timing + cost data to DuckDB/Parquet
task.description:
Modify duckdb_extraction.py to: 1. Add "setup_" and "cost_" to _EXCLUDED_METRIC_PREFIXES tuple (prevents these from appearing in training_metrics table) 2. Add a new extraction function for infrastructure timing params that pulls setup_* and cost_* into a dedicated table
This is a small change — the DuckDB extraction already handles all params generically. We just need to ensure the new prefixes don't pollute training curve data.

tdd_spec.red: - tests/v2/unit/test_duckdb_timing_extraction.py::test_excluded_metric_prefixes_include_setup -> Verify _EXCLUDED_METRIC_PREFIXES contains "setup_" - test_excluded_metric_prefixes_include_cost -> Verify _EXCLUDED_METRIC_PREFIXES contains "cost_" - test_timing_params_extractable -> Create mock MLflow run with setup_* params, verify DuckDB extraction includes them in params table
tdd_spec.green: - Add "setup_" and "cost_" to _EXCLUDED_METRIC_PREFIXES - Verify extraction works with new param prefixes
